security.title: Security Settings
security.ghost_mode_desc: Hides sensitive codes and applies a blur overlay when the app is backgrounded.
security.offline_mode_desc: Enable to physically block all network requests; app runs solely on local synced cache.

security.auth_missing: Local security authorization has expired. Please log in again to ensure this device has offline storage capability.
security.vault_unreachable: Unable to reach account API. Please check your network and try again.
security.insecure_context_blocked: Insecure context detected. Browser has disabled offline cache (HTTPS or localhost required).
security.clipboard_wipe_failed: System restriction: unable to auto-clear clipboard. Please clear it manually.
security.devices_desc: View and manage all logged in devices.

security.confirm_signout_all_desc: Are you sure you want to sign out all other active sessions except the current one? This is a sensitive operation.
security.cannot_remove_current: You cannot remove the device you are currently using
security.pin_lock_desc: Requires a 6-digit PIN verification on app startup or return from background.
security.setup_pin_tip: Set a 6-digit numeric PIN. If forgotten, you must reset the app and re-sync.
security.biometric_unlock_desc: Allow Fingerprint or FaceID to unlock the vault quickly.
security.reset_confirm_message: Resetting will clear all local data. You will need to log in again and sync from the server.
security.disable_pin_title: Verify Identity to Disable Security Lock
security.disable_pin_tip: For security reasons, disabling the lock requires verifying your current 6-digit PIN.
security.pin_verify_failed: PIN verification failed, please try again
security.biometric_hardware_unsupported_msg: Your device supports Passkeys but does not support hardware-level key derivation (PRF extension). For security reasons, biometric quick unlock cannot be enabled. Please continue using PIN protection.
security.auto_lock_delay_desc: If the app is returned from background within this period, it won't require re-authentication.

backup.no_new_accounts: All restored accounts already exist, no new items added.

backup.email_download_not_supported: Email backups cannot be downloaded directly. Please open your email, save the attachment manually, and then use the "Migration" feature to import.
backup.require_email_smtp_password: Please enter auth code or password
backup.email_backup_body_intro:
Hello,

This is your NodeAuth automatic scheduled backup file. Please keep it safe.
backup.email_backup_body_note: Note: The attachment is encrypted with AES-256. Please use your backup password to decrypt and restore it.
backup.gdrive_auth_tip: Back up to your personal Google Drive for full data sovereignty and high availability.
backup.onedrive_auth_tip: Back up to your personal OneDrive for full data sovereignty and high availability.
backup.baidu_auth_tip: Back up to your personal Baidu Netdisk for full data sovereignty and high availability.
backup.dropbox_auth_tip: Back up to your personal Dropbox for full data sovereignty and high availability.
backup.gdrive_folder_tip: Recommended to use alphanumeric paths. e.g., /backups
backup.onedrive_folder_tip: Recommended to use alphanumeric paths. e.g., /backups
backup.baidu_folder_tip: Backup files will be saved to: /apps/nodeauth-backup
backup.token_expired_or_revoked: Authorization has expired. Click "Re-authorize" to reconnect the storage provider.
backup.oauth_token_revoked: Authorization token has expired, please re-authorize.
backup.github_token_invalid_or_revoked: Personal Access Token is invalid or expired. Please enter a valid Token.
backup.telegram_chat_id_tip_1: 1. Private chat back up: Search for userinfobot and send /start to get your ID.
backup.telegram_chat_id_tip_2: 2. Group/Channel back up: Add your Bot to the destination group and grant message sending & deleting permissions, then send any message to get the group ID (usually starts with -100).
backup.file_unavailable: This backup file is unavailable or deleted on Telegram. Do you want to clean up this invalid record?
backup.continue_use_old_pwd: The system will continue to use the previous auto-backup password.
backup.retain_zero_tip: 0 means no limits or cleanup for historical backups.
backup.backup_security_tip: Please enter an encryption password to secure the backup file.
backup.input_custom_pwd: Enter custom encryption password (at least 6 chars)
backup.restore_warning: Restoring will overwrite all current data!
backup.input_restore_pwd: Enter encryption password used for backup
backup.center_desc: Configure multiple backup sources (Email, WebDAV, S3, etc.) to ensure data safety.
backup.delete_warning: This will permanently delete the backup profile (it will NOT affect backed up data in the cloud). Continue?
backup.password_min_length: Password must be at least 6 characters

migration.export_failed: Export failed. Please try again later.

migration.msg_no_new_accounts: ⚠️ All accounts already exist, no new accounts added.
migration.msg_offline_queued: ☁️ Offline Mode: Accounts queued locally and will auto-sync when back online.
migration.export_desc: Select the export format you need. Please note that plaintext exports have security risks.
migration.hub_desc: Manage the import, export, and secure backup of your account data
migration.steam_guard_desc_guide: Supports direct import of .maFile (Steam Desktop Authenticator) or steam:// links. System will auto-detect and apply 5-digit alphanumeric algorithm.
migration.warning_desc: You are exporting unencrypted plaintext data. Anyone with access to this file can access your account codes!
migration.ga_desc_1: Open Google Authenticator on your phone and scan the QR code
migration.ga_desc_multiple: (Too many accounts, generated in batches. Please scan sequentially)
migration.import_desc: Support restoring data from various 2FA apps or system backups.
migration.drag_drop_tip: Drag backup files or migration QR screenshots here, or click to upload
migration.ga_auth_desc: Export 'Transfer QR code' in the App and upload the screenshot (.png / .jpg)
migration.ms_auth_detail: Only support Android, export the following 3 files and upload them together
migration.input_decrypt_pwd_placeholder: Enter the password set during export

tools.apps_review_desc: Mainstream 2FA Applications Review and Anti-Pitfall Guide

2fas.pros: Open source, fully local-first, supports secure iCloud/G-Drive sync.
2fas.cons: Not a true standalone desktop app; you must scan a QR code on PC to use the browser extension.
2fas.summary: Highly trusted local-first 2FA, though the PC experience can be slightly tedious.

aegis.pros: Fully open source, heavy local encryption, wildly powerful export & format compatibility.
aegis.cons: Android-exclusive, lacks native cross-platform cloud sync or an iOS version.
aegis.summary: The absolute top choice for Android privacy geeks and power users.

ente.pros: Fully open source, End-to-End Encrypted cloud sync, offers native desktop apps and a web vault.
ente.cons: Since all data relies on cloud sync, it may lack the purity sought by offline-only purists.
ente.summary: The rapidly growing king of cross-platform sync, serving as Authy's perfect open-source successor.

proton.pros: Deeply integrated into the highly private Proton ecosystem, E2EE sync.
proton.cons: Tied to Proton Pass ecosystem lock-in.
proton.summary: Excellent choice for existing Proton ecosystem users with top-tier privacy.

bitwarden.pros: Open source and free, backed by the reputable Bitwarden security team.
bitwarden.cons: The standalone authenticator app is fairly basic right now (though BW main app also does 2FA).
bitwarden.summary: Pure and solid app endorsed by a reputable giant, ideal for minimalists.

google.pros: Extremely lightweight, works out of the box, offers highly convenient QR batch export, and now supports cloud sync.
google.cons: Closed source. Early sync lacked E2EE (though improved recently). No official desktop app.
google.security: Initial cloud sync was criticized for lacking E2EE, but this has been addressed.
google.summary: A pure, widely-adopted foundation app. Its QR batch export is a killer feature.

microsoft.pros: If you are deep in the Microsoft ecosystem, it supports passwordless logins seamlessly.
microsoft.cons: Severely bloated, forces MS account binding, absolutely refuses standard exports (locking you in), and backups cannot cross iOS/Android.
microsoft.security: Numerous user reports of complete data loss when restoring on a new device, posing a significant security risk.
microsoft.summary: Way too heavy for pure TOTP usage, and its aggressive 'no-export' data monopoly is repulsive (avoid).

freeotp.pros: Open source project maintained by Red Hat, pure and free.
freeotp.cons: Extremely outdated UI, feels abandoned with rare updates.
freeotp.summary: A nostalgic open-source tool, but no longer fits modern daily usage.

authy.pros: Pioneer in cloud sync, very convenient historically.
authy.cons: Closed source, forces phone number binding, holds keys hostage (no exports), desktop apps shut down.
authy.security: Parent company Twilio suffered severe hacker breaches leaking sensitive data.
authy.summary: Once a king, now an absolutely anti-recommended app due to privacy and lock-in risks.

lastpass.pros: Convenient for users deeply tied into LastPass ecosystem.
lastpass.cons: Closed-source cloud architecture, intertwined with LastPass infrastructure.
lastpass.security: LastPass suffered catastrophic global data breaches recently.
lastpass.summary: Due to LastPass's severe reputation crisis, avoid entrusting critical 2FA here.

raivo.pros: Was previously the most recommended open-source iOS 2FA app.
raivo.cons: Recently acquired by a shady shell company (Mobime).
raivo.security: Disastrous update randomly deleted all local seeds for huge amounts of users, unrecoverable.
raivo.summary: A catastrophic tale of trust betrayal and data destruction. DO NOT USE.

api_errors.backup_password_required: Auto backup password is required (at least 6 characters)
api_errors.manual_backup_password_needed: Manual backup with auto-password requires configuring an auto-backup password first.
api_errors.oauth_state_invalid: Invalid or missing OAuth state parameter. Security check failed.
api_errors.not_whitelisted: Access whitelist not configured. Login denied.
api_errors.oauth_token_revoked: Backup storage authorization has expired. Please click the edit icon below to re-authorize.

api_errors.telegram_missing_query: Telegram provider requires full query parameters, not just code

healthCheck.title: Intercept: Security Check Failed
healthCheck.subtitle: We detected critical vulnerabilities or incomplete initialization in the environment configuration. System access is locked to protect your data.
healthCheck.passed_subtitle: Excellent! Your NodeAuth security defense is operating at optimal conditions.

encryption_key_too_short.title: Encryption Key Compliance Error (ENCRYPTION_KEY)
encryption_key_too_short.desc: Your ENCRYPTION_KEY is empty or shorter than 32 characters! This key encrypts all vault seeds and is highly susceptible to brute-forcing.

jwt_secret_too_short.title: Weak Session Secret (JWT_SECRET)
jwt_secret_too_short.desc: Your JWT_SECRET is shorter than 32 characters. Hackers could forge identity credentials and bypass logins.

oauth_allow_all_enabled.title: CRITICAL: Global Access Enabled
oauth_allow_all_enabled.desc: OAUTH_ALLOW_ALL=true is active! This is not a drill. Anyone visiting the site can instantly govern all your 2FA data!

allowed_users_empty.title: Login Whitelist is Empty
allowed_users_empty.desc: The system is locked but OAUTH_ALLOWED_USERS is empty. Nobody can successfully login to this system.

github_config_incomplete.title: Incomplete GitHub Config
github_config_incomplete.desc: You configured GitHub parameters but are missing the Client ID, Secret, or Redirect URI.

telegram_config_incomplete.title: Incomplete Telegram Config
telegram_config_incomplete.desc: Missing Bot Name or Bot Token.

no_provider_configured.title: No OAuth Provider Configured
no_provider_configured.desc: You must configure at least one active OAuth2 provider (like GitHub or Telegram) to access the UI.

suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_worker: One-click Deploy to Worker: Go to Cloudflare Dashboard -> Workers & Pages -> nodeauth -> Settings -> Variables and Secrets
suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_gitaction: Deploy via Github Action: Go to Forked Repository -> Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions
suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_docker: Deploy via Docker Compose: Edit docker-compose.yml -> environment
suggestions.encryption_key_suggestion: Please copy the high-strength random key pre-generated for you below, and set it as ENCRYPTION_KEY according to your deployment method.
suggestions.jwt_secret_suggestion: Please copy the high-strength random key pre-generated for you below, and set it as JWT_SECRET according to your deployment method.
suggestions.oauth_allow_all_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, remove OAUTH_ALLOW_ALL or set its value to false.
suggestions.allowed_users_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_ALLOWED_USERS.
suggestions.github_config_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GITHUB_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.telegram_config_suggestion: Please check the bot configuration in BotFather to ensure the information is complete, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_TELEGRAM_BOT_NAME, OAUTH_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, and OAUTH_TELEGRAM_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.google_config_suggestion: Please check the credentials configuration in Google Cloud Console, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.nodeloc_config_suggestion: Please check your developer credentials in NodeLoc panel, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_NODELOC_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_NODELOC_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_NODELOC_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.gitee_config_suggestion: Please verify Gitee developer credentials, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GITEE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GITEE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GITEE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.cloudflare_config_suggestion: Requires four parameters: Client ID, Secret, Org Domain, Redirect URI. Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.no_provider_suggestion: Consult the README to pick the easiest provider. Retrieve the Client ID and Secret and configure them in Cloudflare's dashboard.

healthCheck.copy_unsupported: Your browser doesn't support automatic copying, please manually select it.
healthCheck.connection_failed: Cannot connect to server for health check

passkey.passkey_desc: Enable passwordless login using biometrics such as fingerprint or face recognition for a more secure and convenient access experience.
passkey.no_passkeys: You haven't registered any Passkeys yet
passkey.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this Passkey? You will no longer be able to login directly with this device.
passkey.add_dialog_tip: Give your device a recognizable name (e.g., My iPhone 15).

emergency.emergency_subtitle: Initialization successful. For your data security, you must back up the core encryption key.
emergency.importance_desc: This system operates on zero-knowledge encryption. The core encryption key is the only credential for your data. In case of server environment loss, it is the only way to recover your data.
emergency.save_instruction: Please download the PDF Core Key on this page and store it in a safe place (e.g., email, cloud storage, or print for safekeeping).
emergency.verify_desc: Please open the PDF file you just downloaded and fill in the last 4 characters of the core key.
emergency.invalid_verification: Verification failed. Please check the last 4 characters of the key in the PDF.
emergency.confirm_success: Core key backup completed, welcome to NodeAuth!
emergency.pdf_self_custody_desc: NodeAuth is a fully self-hosted system. We do not store your master password or encryption keys. This means if you lose this Emergency code, all your data will be permanently locked and irrecoverable.
emergency.pdf_usage_desc: If your server environment variables are lost, fill this key into the ENCRYPTION_KEY variable during redeployment to recover all data.
emergency.pdf_warning: Warning: Never lose this key; it is the only way to recover your data.
